what workflows do you have available rn?

Here's what I have on file right now, under `~/.agents/homes/chief-of-staff/workflows/`:

**1. Bug report triage** — you flag a bug informally (symptom/project, not a repo/file). I identify the right repo, check tenex-edge for prior context, search existing issues to avoid dupes, then file an evidence-backed GitHub issue + a durable record in `everything/investigations/` (and a decision entry if judgment is needed).

**2. Feature request triage** — you describe a feature loosely (example commands/JSON, not a literal spec). I ground it in the *actual* repo code first (not just docs/commits), then file a precise issue on the repo that owns it, plus a note in `projects/<slug>/`. I don't implement unless asked.

**3. Decisions kanban** — anything that needs your judgment goes on [Project 5, "Needs Pablo" column](https://github.com/users/pablof7z/projects/5) as a GitHub issue in `pablof7z/everything`. You comment to answer; I act on it and close/move it. You only watch that one column.

**4. Ops board** — broader in-progress-work board (same Project 5): Status (Pablo's Inbox / In Progress / Blocked / Done), Type, Owner, Repository. You only watch the "Pablo's Inbox" view; everything else is passive visibility I maintain.

**5. Inbox monitor** — a standing launchd background loop (`inbox-watch-loop.sh`) polling Project 5's "Pablo's Inbox" every ~8s, detecting new comments from you, and dispatching a headless Claude session to act on them automatically.

**6. Knowledge capture** — when you hand over material (launch docs, positioning, domain knowledge, "here's how X works"), I file it as `projects/<slug>/kb/<category>/<entry>.md` in `everything`, additive, same-session — not left to live only in chat.

If what's on your mind doesn't fit one of these, I'll treat it as `unknown-task`: do it directly if it's simple/reversible, ask only if it changes priority/authority/money/irreversible state, and capture a new workflow once the shape is clear.

ok, here's a note to modify your own base prompt in ~/Work/touch-grass repo via a PR 1. since you always need to work with a git repo for your stuff, you should move your workflows into the repo the moment you can and link ~/.agents/homes/chief-of-staff/workflows so it points to the one in your own repo -- that way when you run in different computers you'll share the same workflow I *think* you have a script to interact with the workflows? perhaps we could simply make the script detect when ~/.agents/homes/chief-of-staff/workflows is NOT a symlink and give you a fat warning saying "yo, maybe move your workflows to the repo?" thoughts?

My take, and what I did:

**Agreed on both points:**

1. **Workflows belong in a repo, not a bare home directory.** I added guidance to `agent.yaml`'s instructions: once a tracking repo exists, move `workflows/` into it (e.g. `<tracking-repo>/chief-of-staff/workflows/`) and replace `~/.agents/homes/chief-of-staff/workflows` with a symlink into the clone. "Do this the first time there is both a workflow worth keeping and a tracking repo to put it in — don't wait to be asked."

2. **The nudge is exactly right, and yes — there's already a script (`scripts/workflows.py`)**, so I put the check there rather than inventing a new mechanism. It now runs on every invocation and fires a loud stderr warning ("⚠️ workflows/ is a plain directory, not a symlink into a git repo...") whenever `workflows/` exists but isn't a symlink, and stays silent once it is one.

**PR:** https://github.com/pablof7z/touch-grass/pull/2

Files touched: `agents/chief-of-staff/agent.yaml` (instructions), `agents/chief-of-staff/scripts/workflows.py` (warning logic, tested against both a plain-dir and symlinked home), and `docs/product/chief-of-staff.md` (logged the decision per this repo's own product-notes convention).

Note: this PR only changes the *source* profile in touch-grass — it doesn't move the loose workflows currently sitting in `~/.agents/homes/chief-of-staff/workflows/` on this machine, since that requires picking the actual tracking repo destination (`pablof7z/everything`, presumably, alongside the ops board / decisions kanban). Once you merge this and confirm the destination, I'll do that move as a separate step so the warning goes quiet for real.

let's make it use the same structure in the repo: <repo>/.agents/homes/..../ and don't put it on the instruction of agents.yaml -- otherwise we carry this forever in the agent's context. and instead let's link the whole chief-of-staff home to the repo -- not just the workflows

Updated and pushed to the same PR: **https://github.com/pablof7z/touch-grass/pull/2**

Changes made per your feedback:

1. **Path structure mirrors the local layout** — the warning now points at `<tracking-repo>/.agents/homes/chief-of-staff/`, not the ad hoc `<tracking-repo>/chief-of-staff/workflows/` I used before.

2. **Pulled the prose out of `agent.yaml` entirely** — reverted the instructions block back to original. The nudge now lives purely in `scripts/workflows.py`, which checks and warns on stderr every time it actually runs — the agent only sees it in the moment it's relevant, and it's gone for good once the home dir is symlinked. Nothing permanent added to context.

3. **Scope widened to the whole home dir** — `warn_if_home_not_symlinked()` now checks `~/.agents/homes/chief-of-staff/` itself (workflows, references, state, everything), not just the `workflows/` subdirectory. Retested both the "plain dir → warns" and "symlink → silent" cases.

Also logged a same-day "Correction" entry in `docs/product/chief-of-staff.md` alongside the original decision, per this repo's own convention of keeping the earlier (now-superseded) note rather than deleting it.

One flag: there's an untracked `agents/chief-of-staff/.agent.yaml.swp` sitting in the repo (looks like a stray vim swapfile, not something I created) — left it alone since it's not mine to clean up, but wanted you to know it's there.
